P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Tecl!nical Assistance 
•• 
Administration 
•• 
Executive/Legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
(916) 322..5662 
322-.5660 
322·5901 
322~1 
Arthur Bolli 
General Manager 
Humboldt Bay Municipal 
Water District 
P.O. Box 95 
Eureka, CA 
95501 
Dear Mr. Bolli: 
October 15, 1984 
Advice Letter No. A-84-246 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of 
R. H. Nicholson who is a candidate for a position on the 
Humboldt Bay Municipal Water District's Board of Directors. 
FACTS 
Mr. Nicholson is the President of Industrial Electric 
Service Company. 
He owns less than a 10% interest in the 
company. 
Industrial Electric Service Company provides goods and 
services to the District. 
In addition, many clients of 
Industrial Electric Service Company are major wholesale 
customers of the District. 
Two such clients are 
Louisiana-Pacific Pulp Mill and the Simpson Pulp Mill. 
The 
District is currently involved in litigation with these 
companies over water rates and the Board of Directors will be 
faced with many decisions concerning the litigation during the 
next year. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
If Mr. Nicholson is elected to a position on the District's 
Board of Directors, will his financial interest in Industrial 
Electric Service Company prevent him from accepting the 
position? 
CONCLUSION 
If Mr. Nicholson is elected to a position on the District's 
Board of Directors, the Political Reform Act will not prevent 


Arthur Bolli 
October 15, 1984 
Page 2 
him from accepting the position. 
However, the Act may affect 
his governmental decisionmaking. 
DISCUSSION 
The Political Reform Act does not prevent an individual from 
assuming a governmental position. 
However, once the individual 
becomes a public official, the Act may affect his governmental 
decisionmaking. 
Government Code Section 871001/ prohibits a 
public official from making, participating in the making, or in 
any way attempting to use his official position to influence, a 
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he 
has a financial interest. 
An official has a "financial 
interest" within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effec~/ on: 
(a) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect investment worth more 
than one thousand dollars ($1,000) •••• 
(c) 
Any source of income ••• aggregating two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided 
to ••• the public official within 12 months prior to 
the time when the decision is made. 
(d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official is a[nJ ••• officer •••• 
Section 87103. 
Mr. Nicholson holds an investment interest in, and a 
business position with, the company and it is a source of income 
to him. 
If he is elected to a position on the Water District's 
Board, he must disqualify himself on any governmental decision 
which will foreseeably have a material financial effect on 
Industrial Electric Service Company. 
This may include some, or 
all, of the decisions concerning the work that the company 
performs for the District. 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
£/ See the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18700 for the definition of "material financial effect." 


Arthur Bolli 
October 15, 1984 
Page 3 
Because Mr. Nicholson's ownership interest in the company is 
less than 10%, the company's clients will not be treated as 
sources of income to Mr. Nicholson and he will not be required 
to disqualify himself on decisions materially affecting the 
clients. 
For example, Mr. Nicholson will be allowed to make 
decisions concerning the District's litigation with 
Louisiana-Pacific and Simpson, as long as it is not foreseeable 
that these decisions will have a material financial effect on 
the Industrial Electric Service Company. 
The advice contained in this letter is confined to the 
provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
I have not addressed 
the issue of whether Sections 1090 and 1091 apply because these 
provisions are under the jurisdiction of the Attorney General's 
Office. 
If I can be of any help in the future, please feel free 
to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours, 
:J~6~~ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Staff Counsel 
Legal Division 


HUMBOLDT BAY MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT 
828 SEVENTH STREET. P. O. SOX 95 -
EUREKA. CALIFORNIA 95501 
OFFICE 707-443-5018 
ESSEX 707-8222918 
SEPTEMBER 25, 1984 
SOARD OF DIRECTORS 
_. 
~ARSEN, PRESI:JENT 
L~OYD L. rlECATrlORN. \;iC:E-PRES'DENT 
'HAL TER J, HARREN. SECRETARY-TREASURER 
....;Of-!N G. BALiRIEDEL. DiRECTOR 
ALAN N!LSEN. DIRECTOR 
GENERAL MANAGER 
ARTrlUR BO,-LI 
SUPER I NTENDENT 
DONALD :::. REED 
MS. BARBARA MILMAN 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
P.O. BOX 807 
SACRAMENTO,CA 95804 
DEAR MS. MILMAN: 
THE DISTRICT RECENTLY RECEIVED THE ATTACHED REQUEST FROM 
MR. R.H. NICHOLSON WHO IS A CANDIDATE IN THE NOVEMBER 
1984 ELECTION FOR ONE OF OUR DIRECTORIAL DIVISIONS. 
I SPOKE ABOUT THIS REQUEST TO JANICE MCLEAN OF YOUR 
OFFICE VIA TELEPHONE RECENTLY AND SHE SUGGESTED I 
SEND YOU A LETTER SETTING THE MATTER FORTH IN WRITING. 
PER MR. NICHOLSON'S ATTACHED STATEMENTS, HIS DIRECT 
INTEREST IN THE COMPANY IS VERY SMALL (LESS THAN 10%) 
AND HIS DUTIES AS COMPANY PRESIDENT ARE LARGELY AD-
MINISTRATIVE. 
THE CONCERN OVER POTENTIAL CONFLICT SEEMS TO STEM 
PRIMARILY FROM THE FACT THAT MANY OF THE DISTRICT'S 
MAJOR WHOLESALE CUSTOMERS ARE ALSO CUSTOMERS OF 
MR. NICHOLSON'S COMPANY, INDUSTRIAL ELECTRIC SERVICE 
COMPANY. 
FOR EXAMPLE, THE DISTRICT'S TWO LARGEST CUSTOMERS, THE 
LOUISIANA-PACIFIC PULP MILL AND THE SIMPSON PULP MILL, 
WHICH ACCOUNT FOR 80% OF THE DISTRICT'S CONTRACTED 
WATER SALES ARE, ACCORDING TO MR. NICHOLSON, ALSO 
CUSTOMERS OF INDUSTRIAL ELECTRIC. 
THE DISTRICT IS 
PRESENTLY THE DEFENDANT IN A LONG RUNNING COURT 
BATTLE OVER WATER RATES WITH THESE TWO COMPANIES. 
DECISIONS WITH REGARD TO FURTHER COURT ACTION AND/OR 
NEGOTIATIONS, ETC. WILL BE ONE OF THE MAJOR CHALLENGES 
FACING THE BOARD IN THE COMING YEAR. 


HUMBOLDT BAY MUNICIPAL WATER DISTRICT 
BARBARA MILMAN 
SEPTEMBER 177 1984 
PAGE 2 
PLEASE LET US KNOW AS SOON AS POSSIBLE WHETHER 7 GIVEN THE 
ABOVE CIRCUMSTANCES 7 YOU SEE ANY PROBLEM WITH MR. NICHOLSON'S 
CANDIDACY AND POSSIBLE DIRECTORSHIP ON THE DISTRICT'S BOARD. 
PLEASE FEEL FREE TO CALL ME7 MR. NICHOLSON (NUMBER IN ATTACHED 
LETTERHEAD)7 OR THE DISTRICT'S GENERAL COUNSEL 7 RONALD ANGELL 
(707-443-5643) IF ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IS NEEDED. 
AB/FAA 
COPY: 
RONALD ANGELL 
R.H. NICHOLSON 
SINCERELY7 
ARTHUR BOLLI 
GENERAL MANAGER
